reasons: Baxia reduces the value of Esmeralda's shield-and-sustain trading pattern by weakening shield and HP regeneration effects.
He can stay near Esmeralda as a frontline body while the team follows up during her reduced-sustain window.

proof.summary: Esmeralda wants extended shield-based trades, while Baxia can reduce shield and HP regeneration value after applying his mark.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps applying skills during Esmeralda's active trade window.
Allies follow up while Esmeralda's sustain value is reduced.
failureCases: Baxia misses the engage and Esmeralda reaches the backline instead.
Esmeralda avoids extended contact with Baxia and farms shield from easier targets.

reasons: Dyrroth can pressure Esmeralda before she scales into long shield-sustain fights.
His burst and defense-reduction style trades punish Esmeralda when she overextends without enough shield or cooldowns.

proof.summary: Esmeralda wants drawn-out close fights, while Dyrroth can force a shorter burst trade before she stabilizes with shield cycling.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth fights early or mid game before Esmeralda becomes too durable.
Dyrroth commits after Esmeralda uses mobility or shield timing.
failureCases: Dyrroth takes a long trade after his burst window ends.
Esmeralda survives the first combo and turns the fight with shield cycling.

reasons: Karrie's repeated max-HP true damage punishes durable heroes that rely on HP, shields, and defensive value.
Esmeralda can absorb many ordinary damage sources, but repeated Lightwheel Mark triggers reduce her comfort in extended fights.

proof.summary: Esmeralda wants to outlast enemies with shield and durability, while Karrie repeatedly applies max-HP true damage after stacking Lightwheel Mark.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has safe positioning behind peel.
Karrie can keep attacking without being zoned by Esmeralda's dive.
failureCases: Esmeralda reaches Karrie before Karrie can stack marks.
Karrie is under-farmed or lacks protection.

reasons: Lunox can threaten Esmeralda's durability with target-HP scaling magic damage.
She works best when she avoids being dragged into Esmeralda's sustained melee range.

proof.summary: Esmeralda wants long shield-based trades, while Lunox can threaten her with repeated Chaos damage that includes target-HP scaling.
worksBestWhen: Lunox keeps distance and does not let Esmeralda stick to her.
Lunox bursts when Esmeralda's shield or mobility cycle is already committed.
failureCases: Esmeralda closes the gap and forces Lunox to spend defensive tools.
Lunox mistimes the burst and gets dragged into a long fight.

reasons: Valir can keep Esmeralda away with knockback, slow, and repeated zoning.
His value is strongest when he prevents Esmeralda from reaching priority targets instead of trying to kill her alone.

proof.summary: Esmeralda needs to stay close to gain sustained value, while Valir can push and slow her approach to reset spacing.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Esmeralda's real commit.
The team uses the space Valir creates to kite backward or focus another target.
failureCases: Valir wastes knockback before Esmeralda commits.
Esmeralda flanks from fog or reaches Valir while his tools are down.
